title: Build Highway Rush — 3D Endless Traffic Driving Game

requirements.text: Create a new page component at frontend/src/pages/HighwayRushGame.tsx implementing the full Highway Rush game using React Three Fiber and Three.js. The scene must render an endless scrolling highway with realistic road texture, white and yellow lane markings, and a third-person camera positioned slightly behind and above the player's car.

quotes: Endless traffic car driving game (3D-style using Three.js)

acceptanceCriteria: Highway scrolls endlessly without visible seams or stuttering
Camera follows player car from a third-person perspective
Road displays lane markings (white dashed center lines, solid side lines)
Scene renders at smooth 60 FPS on desktop

requirements.text: Implement player car controls: Arrow Left/Right and A/D keys switch lanes on desktop; on mobile, render on-screen left/right touch buttons. The highway must have at least 3 lanes. Lane switching should be smooth with a brief lateral animation.

quotes: Move left and right using Arrow Keys / A & D

acceptanceCriteria: Arrow Left/Right and A/D keys switch lanes
Touch buttons visible and functional on mobile viewports
Lane switch is animated (smooth lateral movement)
Player car cannot move beyond the outermost lanes

requirements.text: Spawn randomized oncoming traffic cars in all lanes with varied colors and simple geometric shapes. Traffic spawn rate and speed increase progressively over time. Collision detection between the player car and any traffic car ends the game.
acceptanceCriteria: Traffic cars spawn in random lanes at random intervals
Traffic speed and spawn frequency increase over time
Collision between player and any traffic car triggers game-over state
No two traffic cars occupy the same lane at the same position simultaneously

requirements.text: Implement a day/night cycle that transitions the sky, ambient lighting, and road appearance over time. At high speeds, apply a motion blur post-processing effect to the scene.

quotes: Motion blur effect when speed increases

acceptanceCriteria: Sky and lighting smoothly transition between day and night states
Motion blur effect activates and intensifies as speed increases
Day/night cycle repeats on a set interval during gameplay

requirements.text: On collision, trigger: a particle explosion effect at the crash point, a screen shake animation, and a brief slow-motion effect. On near-miss (traffic car passes within a threshold distance without collision), award bonus points and briefly trigger a slow-motion effect.

quotes: Slow motion effect on close calls

acceptanceCriteria: Crash triggers particle burst and screen shake
Slow-motion effect activates on crash and on near-misses
Near-miss bonus points are added to the score and displayed briefly
Effects do not permanently alter game state

requirements.text: Display a HUD overlay with: current score (distance-based, incrementing per frame), high score (persisted in localStorage), current speed meter, and coin count. Score and high score must be updated at game over, with the high score saved to localStorage key 'highwayRush_highScore'.

quotes: Score increases based on distance survived
Score display and High Score display

acceptanceCriteria: HUD shows live score, high score, speed, and coin count
High score is loaded from and saved to localStorage on game over
Speed meter reflects current in-game speed value
HUD is readable and non-intrusive over the 3D scene

requirements.text: Spawn collectible coins on the highway that the player can collect by driving over them. Implement a car skins system with at least 3 skins unlockable by spending accumulated coins. The currently selected skin must persist in localStorage.
acceptanceCriteria: Coins spawn on the road and are collected on overlap with player car
Coin count persists across sessions via localStorage
At least 3 car skins available, each with a distinct color/style
Skins can be unlocked and selected from the main menu or a skins screen
Selected skin is applied to the player car in-game

requirements.text: Implement difficulty escalation every 30 seconds: increase traffic density, traffic speed cap, and spawn variety. Show a brief on-screen notification when difficulty increases.
acceptanceCriteria: Difficulty level increments every 30 seconds of gameplay
Each difficulty increment increases traffic density and max speed
An on-screen notification briefly appears on difficulty increase

requirements.text: Implement all game screens: Main Menu (with Play, Car Skins, and Leaderboard/High Score sections), in-game Pause overlay (with Resume and Restart buttons), and Game Over screen (showing final score, high score, coins collected, and Restart/Main Menu buttons). Include a rewarded-ad placeholder on the Game Over screen for 'Extra Life' and an interstitial-ad placeholder zone after dismissing game over.

quotes: Rewarded ad system for extra life

acceptanceCriteria: Main menu renders before game start with Play and Skins options
Pause overlay halts game loop and shows Resume/Restart
Game over screen shows score, high score, coins, restart and menu buttons
Rewarded ad placeholder button visible on game over screen
Interstitial ad placeholder zone shown after game over dismissal
Banner ad placeholder visible at the bottom of the main menu and game over screens

requirements.text: Implement all audio using the Web Audio API (procedural synthesis, no external files): looping engine sound that pitches up with speed, crash/explosion sound on collision, looping background music, and button click sounds. Include a mute toggle button in the HUD, with mute state persisted in localStorage.
acceptanceCriteria: Engine sound loops and pitch-shifts based on current speed
Crash sound plays on collision
Background music loops throughout gameplay
Button click sound plays on all interactive buttons
Mute toggle button in HUD silences all audio
Mute state persists via localStorage

requirements.text: Register the Highway Rush game on the Home page (frontend/src/pages/Home.tsx) as a new game card with an appropriate icon and description, linking to the /highway-rush route. Add the route to the TanStack Router setup in frontend/src/App.tsx.

quotes: Create a high-quality, addictive browser game called 'Highway Rush'

acceptanceCriteria: Highway Rush appears as a card on the Home page
Clicking the card navigates to /highway-rush
The game component loads correctly at that route

requirements.text: Integrate Highway Rush with the existing player profile and XP system (frontend/src/utils/playerProfile.ts): award XP based on distance score at game over, and record win/play stats. Award an achievement for reaching milestone scores (e.g., 1000, 5000, 10000 points).

quotes: Make the game look modern, realistic, and addictive

acceptanceCriteria: XP is awarded at game over proportional to score
Games played counter increments each run
Milestone score achievements unlock and notify via existing achievement system

constraints: Use only Web Audio API for all sound — no external audio files
Use only React Three Fiber / Three.js for 3D rendering — no plain HTML canvas for the game scene
All game state (high score, coins, skin selection, mute) must persist via localStorage only — no backend required
Ad zones must be placeholder UI elements only — no real ad SDK integration
Do not modify any files under frontend/src/hooks/useInternetIdentity.ts, frontend/src/hooks/useActor.ts, frontend/src/main.tsx, or frontend/src/components/ui
nonGoals: Real monetization or live ad network integration
Multiplayer or online leaderboard via backend
